Name: Fog Volume
Description: Defines a volumetric fog region with controllable density, lighting, and noise-based variation. Used to simulate atmospheric effects such as mist, haze, or smoke within a bounded area.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Near Fade: Minimum Distance
Fields.Description: Distance from the camera at which fog begins to fade in. Objects closer than this distance will not be affected by the fog.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Near Fade: Maximum Distance
Fields.Description: Distance from the camera at which fog reaches full intensity after near fade-in.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Edge Fade: Distance
Fields.Description: Controls how far from the volume boundary the fog begins to fade out to avoid hard edges.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Edge Fade: Exponent
Fields.Description: Exponent controlling the smoothness of edge fading. Higher values create a sharper falloff near the boundary.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Height Falloff
Fields.Description: Controls how fog density decreases with height. Higher values make fog thinner at higher elevations.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Fade Distance for Height Map
Fields.Description: Distance over which height-based fog attenuation is blended, useful when using terrain or height maps.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Max Injecting Depth
Fields.Description: Maximum depth into the scene where fog contribution is injected, limiting how far fog affects distant geometry.

Fields.Name: Draw Area: Z Offset
Fields.Description: Offsets the fog volume along the Z axis to adjust its world-space positioning.

Fields.Name: Ray Marching: Max Sample Count
Fields.Description: Maximum number of ray-marching steps used to sample the fog volume. Higher values improve quality but reduce performance.

Fields.Name: Ray Marching: Stepping Distance
Fields.Description: Distance between each ray-marching sample. Smaller values increase accuracy but require more samples.

Fields.Name: Ray Marching: Suspended Transmittance
Fields.Description: Base transmittance value controlling how much light passes through the fog before attenuation is applied.

Fields.Name: Ray Marching: Light Processing Interval
Fields.Description: Interval (in steps) at which lighting calculations are performed during ray marching to optimize performance.

Fields.Name: Ray Marching: Light Processing Interval Threshold
Fields.Description: Threshold distance or density at which reduced light processing intervals are applied to maintain visual quality.

Fields.Name: Light: Overwrite
Fields.Description: If enabled, overrides global scene lighting with the fog volume's custom lighting parameters.

Fields.Name: Light: Light Injecting Type
Fields.Description: Defines the method used to inject light into the fog volume (e.g., directional, local, or hybrid).

Fields.Name: Light: Directional Light Angle
Fields.Description: Direction of the primary light source affecting the fog, typically expressed as azimuth and elevation angles.

Fields.Name: Light: Directional Light Color
Fields.Description: Color and intensity of the primary directional light scattering through the fog.

Fields.Name: Light: Directional Light Opposite Color
Fields.Description: Color of light scattering from the opposite direction, simulating backscattering effects.

Fields.Name: Light: Ambient Color
Fields.Description: Ambient light color contributing to the overall illumination of the fog volume.

Fields.Name: Light: Color Map Texture Intensity
Fields.Description: Controls how strongly the color map texture influences fog lighting.

Fields.Name: Light: Color Map Texture UV Tiling: X
Fields.Description: UV tiling factor along the X axis for the fog lighting color map texture.

Fields.Name: Light: Color Map Texture UV Tiling: Y
Fields.Description: UV tiling factor along the Y axis for the fog lighting color map texture.

Fields.Name: Light: Shadowing Intensity
Fields.Description: Strength of shadowing within the fog. Higher values produce more pronounced volumetric shadows.

Fields.Name: Light: Attenuation Intensity
Fields.Description: Controls how quickly light intensity diminishes as it travels through the fog.

Fields.Name: Light: Attenuation Offset
Fields.Description: Bias applied to light attenuation, shifting where attenuation begins within the fog.

Fields.Name: Light: Octave Number Perlin Noise
Fields.Description: Number of Perlin noise octaves used to modulate lighting variations within the fog.

Fields.Name: Density Intensity
Fields.Description: Overall density multiplier for the fog. Higher values result in thicker, more opaque fog.

Fields.Name: Perlin Noise Frequency
Fields.Description: Frequency of the Perlin noise used to shape fog density. Higher values create finer detail.

Fields.Name: Perlin Noise Lacunarity
Fields.Description: Controls how noise frequency increases across octaves, affecting the complexity of the fog pattern.

Fields.Name: Perlin Noise Gain
Fields.Description: Controls amplitude reduction between noise octaves, influencing contrast in the fog structure.

Fields.Name: Perlin Noise Offset
Fields.Description: Offset applied to the noise sampling position, useful for shifting or animating fog patterns.

Fields.Name: Scroll Speed: X
Fields.Description: Speed at which the fog noise pattern scrolls along the X axis.

Fields.Name: Scroll Speed: Y
Fields.Description: Speed at which the fog noise pattern scrolls along the Y axis.

Fields.Name: Scroll Speed: Z
Fields.Description: Speed at which the fog noise pattern scrolls along the Z axis.

Fields.Name: Scroll Detail Scale
Fields.Description: Scale factor applied to scrolling noise detail, controlling how large or small animated features appear.
